releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This is the first public release of Hledger for Obsidian — a native dashboard for visualizing your hledger journal files
directly inside Obsidian.

### Features

#### Dashboard

- Overview, Expenses, and Income tabs in a native Obsidian view
- Open via ribbon icon or command palette (Open dashboard) 
- Auto-refresh when journal files change

#### Overview Tab

- Summary cards for net worth, income, expenses, and savings — each with a trend indicator vs. the previous period
- Net worth over time (line chart)
- Income vs. expenses monthly comparison (bar chart)
- Expenses by category (pie chart and stacked area chart)
- Account balances with bar visualization
- Recent transactions table

#### Expenses Tab

- Expenses by category (pie chart)
- Daily heatmap — click any day to filter transactions
- Top 5 expenses list
- Full transaction table with search, pagination, and CSV export

#### Income Tab

- Income by source (pie chart)
- Monthly income chart (bar chart)
- Top 5 income sources list
- Full transaction table with search, pagination, and CSV export

#### Filtering & Controls

- Period filter: Month, 12 Months, Year, All, or custom date range
- Commodity/currency selector — auto-detected from your journal
- Account filter to scope the dashboard to a specific account subtree
- Full-text search on transaction descriptions

#### Transaction Entry

- Quick-entry modal with autocomplete for accounts and commodity
- Fields: date, description, account/category, amount, source account
- Writes directly to your journal file

#### Journal Support

- Configurable journal file path (.journal / .ledger)
- Recursive resolution of include directives 
- Multi-commodity support
- Parse error and missing include warnings

#### Settings

- Default period, default commodity, and number of recent transactions to display
- Custom account classification rules (supports English and Portuguese account name prefixes)
- Monthly income chart (bar chart)
- Top 5 income sources list
- Full transaction table with search, pagination, and CSV export

#### Filtering & Controls

- Period filter: Month, 12 Months, Year, All, or custom date range
- Commodity/currency selector — auto-detected from your journal
- Account filter to scope the dashboard to a specific account subtree
- Full-text search on transaction descriptions

#### Transaction Entry

- Quick-entry modal with autocomplete for accounts and commodity
- Fields: date, description, account/category, amount, source account
- Writes directly to your journal file

#### Journal Support

- Configurable journal file path (.journal / .ledger)
- Recursive resolution of include directives
- Multi-commodity support
- Parse error and missing include warnings

#### Settings

- Default period, default commodity, and number of recent transactions to display
- Custom account classification rules (supports English and Portuguese account name prefixes)

#### Theming

- All charts adapt to Obsidian's light and dark themes using CSS variables
- Native Obsidian UI components throughout — no external UI frameworks
